commands.review_summary:
The PR changes session-memory transcript summarization to collapse adjacent identical assistant text and adds regression coverage for delivery-mirror, thinking-cleaned duplicates, distinct replies, and repeated replies after visible user turns.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +129. Total +141 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main appends every extracted user/assistant text row, so a raw thinking assistant entry followed by a cleaned text-only entry with the same visible text produces duplicate memory lines. I did not run a live thinking-enabled model flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is recommended because the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a concrete code defect ClawSweeper can patch.

target.title: fix(session-memory): skip delivery-mirror entries and dedup consecutive identical assistant messages (#92563)
target.body:
## Summary

`getRecentSessionContent` in the session-memory hook would produce duplicate
`assistant:` lines when thinking-enabled models caused two entries to be
written to the session JSONL for the same assistant response.

Two fixes:

1. **Skip `delivery-mirror` entries** — messages with `provider: "openclaw"`
   and `model: "delivery-mirror"` are delivery bookkeeping artifacts that
   duplicate the visible text of the preceding assistant message.

2. **Dedupe consecutive identical assistant messages** — a safety net that
   prevents any other path from producing redundant rows.

Fixes #92563

## Verification

- [x] All 26 existing tests pass (23 original + 3 new)
- [x] New test: delivery-mirror messages are skipped
- [x] New test: consecutive identical assistant messages deduped
- [x] New test: consecutive different assistant messages preserved

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
